claim_boundary.next_gate: If the routing-survival gate passes, preregister a new disjoint-seed layout/readout holdout; otherwise expand or revise the routing-aware objective.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: Acceptance holdout performance, readout mitigation, current calibration, provider access, hardware execution, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or new B10 credit.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Same-condition transpiler-loop ranking of the 60 predeclared R127 candidates against automatic layout on frozen snapshots.

requirements.label: R127 source is hash-bound and all 60 retained candidates are consumed
five declared transpiler seeds are used for every retained candidate
automatic-layout baselines use the same five seeds for all six groups
selection follows the preregistered mean-worst-CX-static-rank order
selected layouts are never worse in mean than static rank one
all 30 selected circuit artifacts preserve logical measurement order
routing-survival acceptance rule is evaluated for every group
R125 acceptance rows, holdout execution, and mitigation remain excluded
historical snapshots remain separate from current and hardware evidence
no soundness, advantage, BQP, or new credit is claimed
